page: Vultr vc2-6c-16gb is an x86_64 architecture cloud server belonging to the Cloud Compute family. It features 6 shared Intel vCPUs mapped to 6 physical cores with 1 thread per core. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 2.67 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a 320 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Because the CPU allocation is shared, this server is designed for workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated CPU performance. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage provides fast read and write speeds for local data access, though overall performance may be subject to resource contention from other tenants on the host. This shared resource model offers a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring a moderate amount of memory and storage without the premium cost of dedicated CPU cores.

This configuration is suitable for general-purpose applications, development environments, staging servers, and lightweight databases. It is also appropriate for web serving, build systems, and other intermittent workloads that can tolerate shared CPU capacity while benefiting from 16.0 GB of RAM and fast NVMe storage.
description: Vultr vc2-6c-16gb is an x86_64 cloud server in the Cloud Compute family, configured with 6 shared Intel vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 320 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating with a shared CPU allocation of 6 physical cores and 1 thread per core, this instance provides a resource-dense option for workloads requiring moderate memory and fast local storage. The server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. Due to the shared nature of the CPU resources, performance may vary depending on host activity, making it a cost-efficient choice compared to dedicated instances. It is best suited for general-purpose tasks, staging environments, web serving, and lightweight database workloads that do not demand sustained, high-intensity compute performance.
